h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: Sorry, right, an actual sentence: “She were late yesterday.” I’d always thought that was just me being sloppy
scored.statedInsightEvidence: that does make it feel more like a rule than a mistake.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to proceed directly to filling out the grammatical grid of home-dialect forms as prompted by the tutor.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought `p < .05` meant the change was important enough to act on, but that is doing too much work.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The dialogue was moving directly toward calculating the operational impact of the new 0.4-second metric.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I think my first instinct was still picturing one big flood doing the main work, because the canyon is so large.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to directly answer the tutor's question about identifying the scar versus the removal route on the rock face.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought “presumed innocent” meant the court was basically starting from “he probably didn’t do it.”
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Now I think the correction is saying that is not really a belief about what happened, but more like a rule about who has to prove the case.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to struggle with the word 'innocent' sounding factual or simply adopt the tutor's suggested phrasing without analyzing their own block.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought fewer predators simply helped the deer because more of them survive, but with the paper deer crowded inside the same chalk boundary, that only explains the first round.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will focus on determining how far to extend the trophic cascade to other species.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The clear water still makes me think the salt is gone, or at least not in there like before.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think the problem with that is I was letting my eyes decide the whole thing and leaving out the scale.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to directly answer the tutor's question about what would be left on the dish when the water dries.
